title: Pirkei Avot §avot_02_07: He used to say: The more flesh, the more worms; The more property, the more a...
body: He used to say: The more flesh, the more worms; The more property, the more anxiety; The more wives, the more witchcraft; The more female slaves, the more lewdness; The more slaves, the more robbery; [But] the more Torah, the more life; The more sitting [in the company of scholars], the more wisdom; The more counsel, the more understanding; The more charity, the more peace. If one acquires a good name, he has acquired something for himself; If one acquires for himself knowledge of Torah, he has acquired life in the world to come.

source.label: Pirkei Avot — Ethics of the Fathers (Mishnah, c. AD 200)

author: engine

witnesses.label: Kaufmann Codex (Budapest, 11-13th cent) — earliest complete Mishnah
Charles Taylor, Sayings of the Jewish Fathers (1877/1897)
